signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry Disadvantage
signals.text: NZD is the weakest G10 currency this week (-1.628% avg) against the strongest USD (+0.767% avg), creating a 2.395% spread that points to further NZD/USD downside. The pair faces significant carry disadvantage with USD offering +150bp over NZD, reinforced by USD's strong positive CESI (+30) and NZD's mildly accommodative real rate (-0.83%). The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 83% hike priced) on 8 Jul is the catalyst to see if a hike bias can offset the strong USD momentum and negative carry. Retail positioning is extremely long NZD (93% long), setting up contrarian unwind risk.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Crowded Short Squeeze Risk + Yield Gap
signals.text: JPY COT net short -112,092 (29.7% long) creates significant short-squeeze risk for USD/JPY, especially as the pair presses multi-decade highs near 162.00. Despite JPY's positive CESI (+17), the substantial +264bp USD/JPY carry advantage for USD and the BoJ's market bias to hold limit JPY's ability to sustain gains. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 31 Jul is the catalyst for any shift in the yield gap or policy stance that is likely to trigger a deeper JPY short squeeze.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Trump Tariff Threat + ECB Hold Bias
signals.text: Trump's threat of 100% tariffs on European countries implementing digital services tax sets up downside pressure for EUR/USD. This geopolitical risk compounds the EUR's net short COT positioning (-40,372) and the ECB's market bias to hold, which limits EUR upside against a restrictive USD (+1.56% real rate). The next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 23 Jul is the catalyst for any policy divergence that could exacerbate or mitigate the tariff-induced weakness.
evidence: Trump says any European country that implements digital services tax will get 100% tariff

signals.title: AUD/USD — Negative CESI + Carry Advantage
signals.text: AUD/USD closed 0.6901 Friday; AUD's moderate negative CESI (-7 misses) suggests underlying data disappointment, which is likely to weigh on the pair. However, AUD maintains a +73bp carry advantage over USD, which provides some support against the negative data flow. The next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 Aug is the catalyst to see if the current hike bias can be sustained despite the weakening economic data.
